lessons.headline: Slow the scan
lessons.lesson: High activity can reduce clarity when too many symbols compete for attention.
lessons.action: Try staying with the top one or two names before opening more.

lessons.headline: Follow repeated attention
lessons.lesson: When you naturally return to the same symbol, it may deserve deeper structured review.
lessons.action: Open the symbol deep dive instead of restarting a broad scan.

lessons.headline: Use mode changes intentionally
lessons.lesson: Changing modes can help, but it works best when you know why you are switching.
lessons.action: Pause and name what problem the new mode is solving before switching again.

lessons.headline: Rotation is becoming a pattern
lessons.lesson: Repeated fast symbol switching may be turning exploration into noise.
lessons.action: Use Focus Mode and commit to a shorter active list.

lessons.headline: USO may deserve a deeper review
lessons.lesson: You are coming back to USO, which may signal stronger conviction than your broader scanning behavior.
lessons.action: Open USO in the symbol deep dive and compare both lanes carefully.

lessons.headline: Mode shifting is showing up in your pattern log
lessons.lesson: Repeated environment changes can either sharpen clarity or fragment it.
lessons.action: Set a default mode for the session before scanning further.
